episodes.title: Episode 9 Defiance Lane: A House, a Trust, and the Cost of Obedience
episodes.description: The story of Sr. Madeleine

episodes.title: Episode 28   Born Inside
episodes.description: In this deeply personal conversation, Bryan Catlin shares what it was like to be born into the Community of Jesus as the Grandson of the Mother and raised entirely within its world. From loneliness, homeschooling, and life with the brothers, to fear, control, emotional confusion, and repeated attempts to leave, Brian reflects on the complexity of growing up inside a high-control religious environment — where even understanding what was “normal” became difficult. This is Part 1 of an honest and powerful survivor story.

episodes.title: Episode 27 8 Months
episodes.description: Note: We have edited the audio to remove the last name of the Community's inside attorney and the Busar mentioned in the podcast. To read the correspondence discussed, go to : 8 Months | My Life During and After The Community of Jesus Carrie Buddington responds to statements made about her departure from the Community of Jesus After living inside the Community of Jesus for 40 years—including 20 years as a vowed sister—Carrie Buddington says she left with a small suitcase, a bus ticket to Boston, and $1,000. In this episode, Carrie responds directly to comments made by attorney Jeff Robbins in a Cape Cod Times article by Denise Coffey published April 29, 2026. She reflects on the years leading up to her departure, repeated requests for a leave of absence that she says were denied, and the difficult process of rebuilding a life after decades inside a high-control religious environment. The title of this episode, 8 Months, refers to the amount of time Carrie says it took to recover a single personal belonging: a cross-stitch piece she had made herself. This is a conversation about autonomy, loss, survival, and what it means to rebuild a life after leaving with almost nothing — and, most of all, about refusing to remain silent in the face of narratives that do not share the full truth.🎧 Listen as we continue telling the story of The Cult on the Cape.

episodes.title: Episode 26 Before We Knew - Part 2
episodes.description: Ken McCormick on surveillance, control, and seeing behind the curtain In Part 2 of Ken McCormick’s story, the deeper mechanisms of control inside the Community of Jesus begin to come into focus. Ken shares what it was like working with the Community’s audio systems—and the unsettling realization that conversations throughout the property could be monitored through hidden microphones and listening systems connected directly to leadership spaces. What began years earlier as a search for meaning and belonging slowly revealed something far more complicated: exhaustion, fear, hierarchy, surveillance, and an environment where privacy and individuality were steadily eroded. This episode explores what happens when spiritual authority becomes intertwined with control—and the moment when people begin to see behind the curtain. 🎧 Listen as we continue telling the story of The Cult on the Cape.

episodes.title: A Special Episode for International Cult Recovery Day
episodes.description: In this special episode for International Cult Recovery Day, Bonnie shares a speech recorded by her son Brendan for a college class final project. In it, Brendan reflects on the Community of Jesus, Rock Harbor Truth, survivor stories, and the importance of listening to people who have too often been silenced or dismissed. This episode is a reminder that recovery begins when people are willing to speak and to hear the truth — and that the next generation is paying attention. 🕊️

episodes.title: Episode 25 Before We Knew
episodes.description: Listen to Ken McCormick share his experiences during the early days of the Community of Jesus

episodes.title: Episode 24 Not A Path, The Path Part 2: From Harm to Healing
episodes.description: What happens after you survive? In this powerful continuation, Bonnie and Jeffrey Wilkinson explore the long road from harm to healing. They talk about shame, identity, self-worth, and the quiet, difficult work of rewriting the messages that were never yours to carry. If you’ve ever struggled with “Who am I now?”—this conversation is for you.

episodes.title: Episode 22 The Truth about Rock Harbor Truth

episodes.title: Remembering Mike Laraja
episodes.description: A Life, A Loss, A Story That Matters

episodes.title: Episode 21   Survival, Evasion, Resistance, and Escape
episodes.description: Matt Hempel entered the Community of Jesus at just 12 years old—and within weeks, he knew something wasn’t right. In this episode, Matt shares what it was like to grow up inside a highly structured, controlled environment where questioning wasn’t welcomed and conformity was expected. While parts of his experience included structure and opportunity, he also witnessed practices that felt confusing, unsettling, and deeply dysfunctional. Looking back, Matt describes those years through a powerful lens: survival, evasion, resistance, and ultimately, escape.

episodes.title: Episode 20 Thirty-Five years, Six months and Two days
episodes.description: Listen to former sister, Dianne Wentworth, reflect on her years inside the Community of Jesus.

episodes.title: Episode 19 Your Host: The Guest
episodes.description: When the storyteller becomes the story. Listen to your host, Bonnie Zampino, tell her story on Let's Get Real, a podcast by Glenda Kroll.

episodes.title: Remembering Aaron Bushnell
episodes.description: Today is the 2-year anniversary of Aaron Bushnell's act of extreme protest, and of his death. Today we aren't discussing the specifics of his final act. Instead, we are remembering the person that Aaron was. Listen to letters and stories from his friends, his teachers, and those who grew up with Aaron and his family at Grenville Christian College and the Community of Jesus. Thank you to Beth, Mel, Madeleine, Hannah and Erin. Aaron's family loves him and misses him very much.
